steps.title: Support the no-role conclusion
steps.content: Sato and colleagues concluded that neither V. fischeri nor B. fragilis likely played a significant role in multicellular development. Since the study concerns whether those bacteria cause S. rosetta to form colonies, supporting evidence should show that neither bacterium tends to trigger colony formation.

steps.title: Reject colony triggers
steps.content: Choice A says both bacteria triggered colonies, which weakens the conclusion. Choice B says V. fischeri did trigger colonies. Choice C says B. fragilis did trigger colonies. Only a finding that neither exposure tended to produce colonies supports ruling out both bacteria. The answer is D.
